## Callbacks

All callbacks are optional. Define any in `main.lua` and the engine calls them automatically.

```lua
function lurek.load()  -- Called once after the script is loaded.
function lurek.update( dt : number )  -- Called every frame; dt = elapsed seconds.
function lurek.draw()  -- Called every frame for rendering.
function lurek.keypressed( key : string, scancode : string, isrepeat : boolean )  -- Key press event.
function lurek.keyreleased( key : string, scancode : string )  -- Key release event.
function lurek.textinput( text : string )  -- Unicode character typed.
function lurek.mousepressed( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button press.
function lurek.mousereleased( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button release.
function lurek.wheelmoved( x : number, y : number )  -- Mouse wheel scroll.
function lurek.input.gamepadpressed( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button press.
function lurek.input.gamepadreleased( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button release.
function lurek.input.gamepadaxis( id : number, axis : string, value : number )  -- Gamepad axis; value in -1..1.
function lurek.joystickadded( id : number )  -- Gamepad connected.
function lurek.joystickremoved( id : number )  -- Gamepad disconnected.
function lurek.touchpressed( id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch begin.
function lurek.touchmoved(  id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch move.
function lurek.touchreleased(id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch end.
function lurek.focus( focused : boolean )  -- Window focus change.
function lurek.visible( visible : boolean )  -- Window show/hide.
function lurek.resize( w : number, h : number )  -- Window resized.
function lurek.quit()  -- Return true to cancel quit.
function lurek.errorhandler( msg : string )  -- Unhandled Lua error.
```

### `Combo`

Lua-side wrapper for a [`ComboDetector`] with an integrated millisecond clock.

```lua
Combo:feed( key : string ) -> nil  -- Feed a key-press event into the combo detector
Combo:getStep( index : integer ) -> nil  -- Returns the step at the given 1-based index as `{key=..., gap_ms=...}`
Combo:isInProgress() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the detector is currently mid-sequence
Combo:progress() -> integer  -- Returns the number of steps matched so far (0 when idle)
Combo:reset() -> nil  -- Reset the detector to its initial idle state, cancelling any in-progress sequence
Combo:tick( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advance the internal clock by `dt` seconds and check for timeouts
Combo:totalSteps() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of steps in the combo sequence
```

### `Cursor`

Lua-side wrapper around a mouse cursor handle.

```lua
Cursor:getType() -> string  -- Returns the cursor type as "system" or "custom"
Cursor:release() -> nil  -- Releases the cursor resource (no-op on desktop)
```

### `InputRecording`

Lua userdata wrapper for a completed [`crate::input::recorder::InputRecording`].

```lua
InputRecording:frameCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of sparse event frames stored in this recording
InputRecording:toJson() -> string  -- Serializes this recording to a JSON string for saving to disk
InputRecording:totalFrames() -> integer  -- Returns the total frame count when recording was stopped
```

## `lurek.timer` {#timer}

> `lurek.timer` - Frame timing, FPS tracking, and scheduled Lua callbacks.

*Coverage: 43/43 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.timer.afterReal( delay : number, func : function ) -> nil  -- Schedules a one-shot callback that fires after `delay` wall-clock seconds,
lurek.timer.chain( steps : table ) -> Scheduler  -- Creates a new Scheduler loaded with a sequenced one-shot chain
lurek.timer.getAverageDelta() -> number  -- Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds
lurek.timer.getDelta() -> number  -- Returns the delta time in seconds for the current frame
lurek.timer.getFPS() -> number  -- Returns the current frames-per-second measurement
lurek.timer.getFrameCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of frames rendered since engine start
lurek.timer.getMicroTime() -> number  -- Returns the high-resolution elapsed time since engine start in seconds
lurek.timer.getPhysicsDelta() -> number  -- Returns the fixed timestep used by `process_physics` callbacks (seconds)
lurek.timer.getPhysicsMaxSteps() -> integer  -- Returns the maximum number of physics sub-steps allowed per frame
lurek.timer.getSmoothedDelta() -> number  -- Returns the exponential moving-average of frame deltas in seconds
lurek.timer.getTime() -> number  -- Returns the total elapsed time since engine start in seconds
lurek.timer.newScheduler() -> Scheduler  -- Creates a new independent Scheduler for managing timed callbacks
lurek.timer.setPhysicsDelta( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the fixed timestep for `process_physics` callbacks (seconds)
lurek.timer.setPhysicsMaxSteps( n : integer )  -- Sets the maximum number of physics sub-steps allowed per frame (clamped 1–64)
lurek.timer.setSmoothingFactor( alpha : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the smoothing factor (alpha) for `getSmoothedDelta`. Must be in [0.01, 1.0]
lurek.timer.sleep( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Suspends execution for the given number of seconds
lurek.timer.step() -> number  -- Advances the timer by one frame, returning the delta time
lurek.timer.tickRealTimers() -> table|nil  -- Advances all real-time timers by one tick; called automatically each frame
lurek.timer.tickWaits() -> table|nil  -- Advances all `lurek.timer.wait()` coroutines by one tick; called each frame
lurek.timer.waitFrames( frames : integer ) -> nil  -- Yields the current Lua coroutine for at least `frames` engine frames
lurek.timer.waitSeconds( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Yields the current Lua coroutine for at least `seconds` wall-clock seconds
```

### `Scheduler`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Scheduler`] with per-event callback storage.

```lua
Scheduler:after( delay : number, func : function ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire once after a delay
Scheduler:afterFrames( n : integer, func : function ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire once after `n` frames
Scheduler:cancel( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a scheduled event by its numeric ID
Scheduler:cancelAll() -> integer  -- Cancels all scheduled events and returns the count removed
Scheduler:cancelNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a scheduled event by its string name
Scheduler:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of active scheduled events
Scheduler:getInterval( id : integer ) -> number?  -- Returns the base interval in seconds for an event, or nil
Scheduler:getRemaining( id : integer ) -> number?  -- Returns the seconds remaining until the next fire for an event, or nil
Scheduler:getRepeatCount( id : integer ) -> integer?  -- Returns the repeat count remaining for an event, or nil
Scheduler:getTimeScale() -> number  -- Returns the current time-scale multiplier
Scheduler:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the scheduler has no active events
Scheduler:isPaused( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given event is currently paused
Scheduler:isPausedNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the named event is currently paused
Scheduler:pause( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Pauses a scheduled event by its ID
Scheduler:pauseNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Pauses a scheduled event by its string name
Scheduler:resetEvent( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Resets an event's remaining time back to its original interval
Scheduler:resume( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Resumes a paused event by its ID
Scheduler:resumeNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Resumes a paused event by its string name
Scheduler:setInterval( id : integer, interval : number ) -> boolean  -- Changes the repeat interval of an existing event
Scheduler:setTimeScale( scale : number ) -> nil  -- Sets a global time-scale multiplier for this scheduler
Scheduler:update( dt : number ) -> integer  -- Advances all timers by dt seconds, firing due callbacks
Scheduler:updateFrames() -> integer  -- Advances frame-based events by one frame, firing due callbacks
```

## `lurek.math` {#math}

> `lurek.math` — Math utilities: random generators, transforms, Bezier curves, tweening,
> spatial hashing, noise, easing, polygon triangulation, and color-space conversion.

*Coverage: 204/204 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.math.Vec2( x : number, y : number )  -- Compatibility alias for `vec2`
lurek.math.Vec3( x : number, y : number, z : number )  -- Compatibility alias for `vec3`
lurek.math.aabbTree() -> AabbTree  -- Creates a new empty AABB tree for efficient broad-phase overlap queries
lurek.math.abs( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the absolute value of x
lurek.math.acos( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the arccosine of x, in radians
lurek.math.angleBetween( x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> number  -- Returns the angle in radians from (x1, y1) to (x2, y2)
lurek.math.applyEasing( name : string, t : number ) -> number  -- Applies a named easing function to progress value t
lurek.math.asin( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the arcsine of x, in radians
lurek.math.atan( y : number, x : number? ) -> number  -- Returns the arctangent of x (or atan2(y, x) when two args given)
lurek.math.atan2( y : number, x : number ) -> number  -- Returns atan(y/x) using the signs of both args to determine the quadrant
lurek.math.bresenham( x1 : integer, y1 : integer, x2 : integer, y2 : integer ) -> table  -- Rasterizes a line from (x1,y1) to (x2,y2) using Bresenham's algorithm. Returns a table of {x,y} tables
lurek.math.catmullRom( points : table ) -> CatmullRomSpline  -- Creates a Catmull-Rom spline through the given control points
lurek.math.ceil( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the smallest integer ≥ x
lurek.math.circleContainsPoint( cx : number, cy : number, r : number, px : number, py : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the point (px, py) lies inside the circle
lurek.math.circleIntersectsCircle( x1 : number, y1 : number, r1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number, r2 : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if two circles overlap
lurek.math.circleIntersectsLine( cx : number, cy : number, r : number, lx1 : number, ly1 : number, lx2 : number, ly2 : number ) -> table  -- Tests an infinite line against a circle. Returns hit, then two optional hit-point pairs
lurek.math.circleIntersectsSegment( cx : number, cy : number, r : number, sx1 : number, sy1 : number, sx2 : number, sy2 : number ) -> table  -- Tests a line segment against a circle. Returns hit, then two optional hit-point pairs
lurek.math.clamp( x : number, lo : number, hi : number ) -> number  -- Returns x clamped to [lo, hi]
lurek.math.clamp( v : number, min : number, max : number ) -> number  -- Clamps `v` between `min` and `max`
lurek.math.closestPointOnSegment( px : number, py : number, x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> number  -- Returns the closest point on segment (x1,y1)-(x2,y2) to point (px,py)
lurek.math.convexHull( points : table ) -> table  -- Computes the convex hull of a flat {x1,y1,...} point list. Returns a flat table
lurek.math.cos( x : number ) -> number  -- Returns the cosine of x (radians)
lurek.math.deg( rad : number ) -> number  -- Converts radians to degrees
lurek.math.delaunayTriangulate( points : table ) -> table  -- Delaunay triangulation of a flat {x1,y1,...} point list. Returns a table of flat 6-number triangle tables
```

### `AabbTree`

```lua
AabbTree:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries in the tree
AabbTree:queryPoint( x : number, y : number ) -> table  -- Returns the ids of all entries whose AABBs contain the given point
AabbTree:remove( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes the entry with the given id
```

```lua
BezierCurve:scale( s : number, ox : number, oy : number ) -> nil  -- Scales all control points around a pivot by factor s
```

## `lurek.audio` {#audio}

> `lurek.audio` — Audio playback, mixing, MIDI synthesis, and DSP effects.
>
> Registers sources (streamed or static), bus-based mixing, MIDI playback,
> streaming decoders, `SoundData` buffers, and spatial audio positioning.
> All mixer state lives in [`SharedState`].

*Coverage: 212/212 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.audio.add_effect( bus_name : string, effect_type : string, params : table ) -> integer  -- Adds a DSP effect to a bus
lurek.audio.applyBandpass( sounddata : SoundData, low_hz : number, high_hz : number ) -> nil  -- Applies a bandpass filter (high-pass then low-pass) to a SoundData in-place
lurek.audio.applyGain( sounddata : SoundData, gain : number ) -> nil  -- Scales every sample by gain (clamped to [-1, 1])
lurek.audio.applyHighpass( sounddata : SoundData, cutoff_hz : number ) -> nil  -- Applies a first-order IIR high-pass filter to a SoundData in-place
lurek.audio.applyLowpass( sounddata : SoundData, cutoff_hz : number ) -> nil  -- Applies a first-order IIR low-pass filter to a SoundData in-place
lurek.audio.clearFilter( source : Source ) -> nil  -- Removes any active filter from a source
lurek.audio.clearMidiSoundFont() -> nil  -- Unloads the active SoundFont
lurek.audio.clearRandomPitch( src : AudioSource ) -> nil  -- Clears any random pitch range on a source, restoring fixed pitch
lurek.audio.clone( source : Source ) -> Source  -- Creates an independent copy of a source
lurek.audio.create_bus( name : string, parent_name : string ) -> nil  -- Creates a bus by name (functional style)
lurek.audio.crossfade( from : AudioSource, to : AudioSource, duration : number ) -> nil  -- Crossfades from one source to another over a duration
lurek.audio.fadeIn( source : Source, dur : number ) -> nil  -- Fades a source in from silence over the given duration
lurek.audio.getActiveSourceCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of currently playing sources
lurek.audio.getBusPeak( bus_name : string ) -> number  -- Returns the peak signal level of the named bus (stub: always 0.0)
lurek.audio.getBusRms( bus_name : string ) -> number  -- Returns the RMS signal level of the named bus (stub: always 0.0)
lurek.audio.getDistanceModel() -> string  -- Returns the current distance model name
lurek.audio.getDopplerScale() -> number  -- Returns the current Doppler scale
lurek.audio.getDuration( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the total duration of a source in seconds
lurek.audio.getFadeIn( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the fade-in duration of a source
lurek.audio.getFreeBufferCount( qsource_id : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the free buffer slots in a queueable source
lurek.audio.getHighpass( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the high-pass filter cutoff of a source
lurek.audio.getListener() -> number  -- Returns the 3D listener position (x, y, z)
lurek.audio.getListener2D() -> number  -- Returns the 2D listener position (x, y)
lurek.audio.getLowpass( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the low-pass filter cutoff of a source
lurek.audio.getMasterVolume() -> number  -- Returns the global master volume
lurek.audio.getMaxSources() -> integer  -- Returns the maximum number of simultaneous sources
lurek.audio.getMeter() -> table|nil  -- Returns the stored master peak meter level
lurek.audio.getOrientation( source : Source ) -> table|nil  -- Returns the 6-component orientation of a source
lurek.audio.getPan( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the source stereo panning
lurek.audio.getPitch( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the source pitch multiplier
lurek.audio.getPlaybackDevice() -> string  -- Returns the current audio output device name
lurek.audio.getPlaybackDevices() -> table  -- Returns a table of available audio output device names
lurek.audio.getPosition( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the 3D position of a source (x, y, z)
lurek.audio.getSourceBus( source : Source ) -> Bus  -- Returns the bus a source is assigned to, or nil
lurek.audio.getSourceCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of registered sources
lurek.audio.getSourceType( source : Source ) -> string  -- Returns the type string ("static" or "stream") of a source
lurek.audio.getStereoWidth( src : AudioSource ) -> number  -- Returns the current stereo width for a source
lurek.audio.getVelocity( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the velocity of a source (x, y, z)
lurek.audio.getVolume( source : Source ) -> number  -- Returns the source volume
lurek.audio.hasMidiSoundFont() -> boolean  -- Returns true if a SoundFont is loaded
lurek.audio.isLooping( source : Source ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if looping is enabled
lurek.audio.isPaused( source : Source ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the source is paused
lurek.audio.isPlaying( source : Source ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the source is playing
lurek.audio.isStopped( source : Source ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the source is stopped
lurek.audio.mixInto( dest : SoundData, src : SoundData ) -> nil  -- Additively mixes another SoundData into the destination in-place
lurek.audio.newBus( name : string ) -> Bus  -- Creates a named audio bus for grouping sources
lurek.audio.newDecoder( source : string, buffersize : integer ) -> Decoder  -- Creates a streaming audio decoder
lurek.audio.newMidiPlayer( path : string ) -> MidiPlayer  -- Creates a MIDI player, optionally loading a file
lurek.audio.newPool( file_path : string, voice_count : integer ) -> SoundPool  -- Creates a polyphonic sound pool for the given file with N simultaneous voices
lurek.audio.newQueueableSource( sample_rate : integer, bit_depth : integer, channels : integer, buffer_count : integer ) -> integer  -- Creates a queueable source for manual PCM buffering
lurek.audio.newSawtoothWave( freq : number, duration : number, sampleRate : number, amplitude : number ) -> SoundData  -- Generate a mono sawtooth-wave SoundData buffer
```

### `Bus`

Lua-side wrapper for an audio bus resource.

```lua
Bus:clearDuck() -> nil  -- Removes the ducking target from this bus, restoring the target bus
Bus:getName() -> string  -- Returns the unique name string assigned to this audio bus
Bus:getPeak() -> nil  -- Returns the average peak amplitude of all sources currently on this bus
Bus:getPitch() -> number  -- Returns the bus pitch multiplier
Bus:getVolume() -> number  -- Returns the current volume multiplier applied to all sources on this bus
Bus:isPaused() -> boolean  -- Returns true if this bus is paused
Bus:pause() -> nil  -- Pauses all sources on this bus
Bus:resume() -> nil  -- Resumes all sources on this bus
Bus:setPitch( pitch : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the pitch multiplier for all sources on this bus
Bus:setVolume( vol : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the volume for all sources on this bus
Bus:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Bus:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```


## `lurek.physics` {#physics}

> `lurek.physics` — Rigid-body physics simulation, collision detection, joints, and raycasting.

*Coverage: 160/160 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.physics.attachShape( body : Body, shape : Shape ) -> nil  -- Attaches a standalone shape to a body as an additional fixture
lurek.physics.debugDraw( enable : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables the physics debug overlay (AABB boxes and velocity vectors)
lurek.physics.destroyWorld( world : World ) -> nil  -- Marks a physics world for destruction. Subsequent operations on the world
lurek.physics.drawDebugGpu( world : World, config : table|nil ) -> nil  -- Extracts collider geometry from a World and queues a GPU physics debug
lurek.physics.getBody( world : World, body : Body ) -> number  -- Returns the position and velocity of a body (x, y, vx, vy)
lurek.physics.getCollisions( world : World ) -> table  -- Returns all collision events from the last simulation step
lurek.physics.isSleepingAllowed( world : World, body : Body ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the body is allowed to sleep
lurek.physics.newBody( world : World, x : number, y : number, bodyType : string ) -> Body  -- Creates a new rectangular body in the given world
lurek.physics.newCellular( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LuaCellular  -- Creates a falling-sand cellular automaton grid
lurek.physics.newChainShape( closed : boolean ) -> Shape  -- Creates a chain shape userdata from flat variadic vertex pairs
lurek.physics.newCircleShape( radius : number ) -> Shape  -- Creates a circle shape userdata
lurek.physics.newEdgeShape( x1 : number, y1 : number, x2 : number, y2 : number ) -> Shape  -- Creates an edge (line segment) shape userdata
lurek.physics.newPolygonShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a convex polygon shape userdata from flat variadic vertex pairs
lurek.physics.newRectangleShape( width : number, height : number ) -> Shape  -- Creates a rectangle shape userdata
lurek.physics.newTerrain( width : integer, height : integer, cell_size : number, world_handle : LuaWorld ) -> LuaTerrain  -- Creates a destructible terrain grid
lurek.physics.newWorld( gx : number, gy : number ) -> World  -- Creates a new physics world with the given gravity vector
lurek.physics.setBodyVelocity( world : World, body : Body, vx : number, vy : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the velocity of a body
lurek.physics.setSleepingAllowed( world : World, body : Body, allowed : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets whether the body is allowed to sleep
lurek.physics.step( world : World, dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances the physics world by dt seconds
```

### `Body`

Lua-side handle to a physics body accessed through its world.

```lua
Body:applyAngularImpulse( impulse : number ) -> nil  -- Applies an angular impulse
Body:applyForce( fx : number, fy : number ) -> nil  -- Applies a continuous force to the body
Body:applyImpulse( ix : number, iy : number ) -> nil  -- Applies a linear impulse to the body
Body:applyTorque( torque : number ) -> nil  -- Applies a torque (rotational force)
Body:destroy() -> nil  -- Removes this body from the world
Body:getAngle() -> number  -- Returns the body angle in radians
Body:getAngularDamping() -> number  -- Returns the angular damping coefficient
Body:getAngularVelocity() -> number  -- Returns the angular velocity in radians/s
Body:getFriction() -> number  -- Returns the body friction coefficient
Body:getGravityScale() -> number  -- Returns the per-body gravity multiplier
Body:getHeight() -> number  -- Returns the height of this body's primary collider shape in world units
Body:getId() -> integer  -- Returns the body's integer ID
Body:getLayer() -> integer  -- Returns the collision layer bitmask
Body:getLinearDamping() -> number  -- Returns the linear damping coefficient
Body:getMask() -> integer  -- Returns the collision mask bitmask
Body:getMass() -> number  -- Returns the body mass in kilograms used for force and impulse calculations
Body:getPosition() -> number  -- Returns the body position (x, y)
Body:getRestitution() -> number  -- Returns the body restitution (bounciness)
Body:getType() -> string  -- Returns the body type as a string
Body:getVelocity() -> number  -- Returns the body velocity (vx, vy)
Body:getWidth() -> number  -- Returns the width of this body's primary collider shape in world units
Body:getX() -> number  -- Returns the body X position
Body:getY() -> number  -- Returns the body Y position
Body:isBullet() -> boolean  -- Returns whether CCD is enabled
Body:isFixedRotation() -> boolean  -- Returns whether rotation is locked
Body:isSleeping() -> boolean  -- Returns true if this body is currently sleeping (inactive)
Body:isSleepingAllowed() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the body can sleep
Body:setAngle( angle : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the body angle in radians
Body:setAngularDamping( damping : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the angular damping coefficient
Body:setAngularVelocity( omega : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the angular velocity
Body:setBullet( bullet : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables continuous collision detection (CCD) for fast-moving bodies
Body:setFixedRotation( fixed : boolean ) -> nil  -- Locks or unlocks rotation
Body:setFriction( friction : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the body friction coefficient
Body:setGravityScale( scale : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the per-body gravity multiplier
Body:setLayer( layer : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the collision layer bitmask
Body:setLinearDamping( damping : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the linear damping coefficient
Body:setMask( mask : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the collision mask bitmask
Body:setMass( mass : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the body mass; affects how forces and impulses change velocity
Body:setPosition( x : number, y : number ) -> nil  -- Teleports the body to the given world-space position, bypassing collision
Body:setRestitution( restitution : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the body restitution (bounciness)
Body:setSleepingAllowed( allowed : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets whether the body can sleep
Body:setType( bodyType : string ) -> nil  -- Changes the body type: `"dynamic"`, `"static"`, or `"kinematic"`
Body:setVelocity( vx : number, vy : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the body's linear velocity in world units per second
Body:sleep() -> nil  -- Puts this body to sleep immediately
Body:wakeUp() -> nil  -- Forcibly wakes up this body
```

## `lurek.event` {#event}

> `lurek.event` — Event queue polling and pub-sub signal dispatching.
>
> Provides `Signal` userdata for named-event pub-sub with once-fire handles,
> per-handle filter predicates, wildcard subscriptions, and integration with the
> engine event bus for built-in events (resize, focus, quit, etc.).

*Coverage: 22/22 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.event.clear() -> nil  -- Discards all pending events in the queue
lurek.event.clearHistory() -> nil  -- Clears all recorded event history
lurek.event.enableHistory( capacity : integer ) -> nil  -- Enables event history recording, keeping the last `capacity` pushed events
lurek.event.exit( code : integer? ) -> nil  -- Pushes an exit event, requesting the engine to stop
lurek.event.flushDeferred() -> table|nil  -- Moves all buffered deferred events into the main event queue and clears the buffer
lurek.event.getHistory() -> table  -- Returns an array of recent events as `{name, args}` tables
lurek.event.newSignal() -> Signal  -- Creates a new pub-sub Signal dispatcher
lurek.event.poll() -> function  -- Returns an iterator function that pops events from the queue
lurek.event.pump() -> nil  -- Syncs OS-level events into the queue (no-op in Lurek2D push model)
lurek.event.push( args : MultiValue )  -- Adds an event item to the end of the event queue for processing
lurek.event.pushDeferred( name : string ) -> nil  -- Pushes a named event to the deferred buffer; it will not reach the main queue
lurek.event.quit() -> nil  -- Alias for `exit()` — requests the engine to stop at the end of the current frame
lurek.event.restart() -> nil  -- Requests that the engine restart at the beginning of the next frame
lurek.event.wait( timeout : number? ) -> string?  -- Blocks until the next event arrives or the optional timeout elapses
```

## `lurek.thread` {#thread}

> `lurek.thread` — Background threads and inter-thread channel communication.


### `Channel`

A synchronized message queue for cross-VM communication.

```lua
Channel:clear() -> nil  -- Clears all items from the channel
Channel:demand( timeout : number? ) -> string|number|boolean|table|nil  -- Blocks until a value is available or the timeout expires, then removes and returns it
Channel:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the channel
Channel:peek() -> string|number|boolean|table|nil  -- Retrieves the value from the channel without removing it
Channel:pop() -> string|number|boolean|table|nil  -- Retrieves and removes a value from the channel
Channel:popBytes() -> string?  -- Pops a bytes value from the channel and returns it as a Lua string
Channel:popTable() -> table?  -- Pops a value from the channel expecting a table
Channel:push( value : any ) -> integer  -- Pushes a value to the channel
Channel:pushBytes( data : string ) -> integer  -- Pushes raw binary data (a Lua string treated as a byte array) to the channel
Channel:pushTable( value : table ) -> integer  -- Serializes a Lua table and pushes it to the channel
Channel:supply( value : any ) -> nil  -- Blocks until the channel has space, then adds the value
Channel:type() -> string  -- Returns the type of the object
Channel:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Checks if the object is of the specified type
```

### `Promise`

Lua-side wrapper around a one-shot [`Promise`].

```lua
Promise:getError() -> string?  -- Returns the worker error string if the promise failed, otherwise nil
Promise:isDone() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the promise has a result or has errored (non-blocking)
Promise:result() -> table|nil  -- Pops and returns the promise result, or nil if not yet ready
Promise:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Promise:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this object is of the given type
```

### `ThreadHandle`

Lua-side wrapper around a background [`LuaThread`].

```lua
ThreadHandle:getError() -> string?  -- Returns the error message if the thread failed, or nil
ThreadHandle:isRunning() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the thread is currently executing
ThreadHandle:start( args : MultiValue ) -> nil  -- Launches the background thread, passing optional arguments via varargs
ThreadHandle:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
ThreadHandle:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this object is of the given type
ThreadHandle:wait() -> nil  -- Blocks the calling thread until the background thread finishes
```

### `ThreadPool`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`ThreadPool`].

```lua
ThreadPool:collect() -> table|nil  -- Retrieves the next result from the pool's output channel (non-blocking)
ThreadPool:getInputChannel() -> Channel  -- Returns the shared input Channel (main → workers)
ThreadPool:getOutputChannel() -> Channel  -- Returns the shared output Channel (workers → main)
ThreadPool:join() -> nil  -- Blocks until all workers in the pool have finished execution
ThreadPool:size() -> integer  -- Returns the number of workers in this pool

```

```lua
lurek.ai.newContextSteering( slots : integer ) -> ContextSteering  -- Creates a new context steering controller

lurek.ai.newGOAPPlanner() -> GOAPPlanner  -- Creates a new GOAP planning solver
```

---

## `lurek.compute` {#compute}

> `lurek.compute` — Dense N-dimensional numerical arrays with NumPy-style operations.
>
> Wraps [`NdArray`] as `Array` userdata supporting element-wise arithmetic, broadcasting,
> reshape/transpose/slice, reduction (sum, mean, min, max), linear algebra (matmul, dot,
> norm), spatial queries (nearest, radius search), and statistical analytics.

*Coverage: 67/67 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.compute.affine2d( tx : number, ty : number, angle_rad : number, sx : number, sy : number ) -> Array  -- Creates a 3×3 homogeneous affine matrix
lurek.compute.fft( samples : table ) -> table  -- Computes the discrete Fourier transform of a 1D real-valued sample array
lurek.compute.fftMagnitude( samples : table ) -> table  -- Returns the magnitude spectrum `|X[k]|` of a real-valued sample array
lurek.compute.fromTable( data : table, shape : table?, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates an array from a Lua table of numbers with optional shape and dtype
lurek.compute.gaussianKernel( size : integer, sigma : number ) -> Array  -- Creates a size×size Gaussian kernel array
lurek.compute.ifft( freqs : table ) -> table  -- Computes the inverse discrete Fourier transform
lurek.compute.newArray( shape : table, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a zero-initialized array with the given shape and optional dtype
lurek.compute.ones( shape : table, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a one-filled array with the given shape and optional dtype
lurek.compute.range( start : number, stop : number, step : number?, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a 1D array from start to stop with optional step and dtype
lurek.compute.rotate2dMatrix( angle_rad : number ) -> Array  -- Creates a 2×2 rotation matrix for the given angle in radians
lurek.compute.zeros( shape : table, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a zero-filled array with the given shape and optional dtype
```

### `Array`

Lua-side wrapper around [`NdArray`].

```lua
Array:abs() -> Array  -- Element-wise absolute value
Array:all() -> boolean  -- Returns true if all elements are nonzero
Array:any() -> boolean  -- Returns true if any element is nonzero
Array:argmax() -> integer  -- Returns the 1-based flat index of the maximum element
Array:argmin() -> integer  -- Returns the 1-based flat index of the minimum element
Array:bitwiseAnd( other : Array ) -> Array  -- Bitwise AND of two Int32 arrays
Array:bitwiseLShift( amount : integer ) -> Array  -- Bitwise left shift of an Int32 array
Array:bitwiseNot() -> Array  -- Bitwise NOT of an Int32 array
Array:bitwiseOr( other : Array ) -> Array  -- Bitwise OR of two Int32 arrays
Array:bitwiseRShift( amount : integer ) -> Array  -- Bitwise right shift of an Int32 array
Array:bitwiseXor( other : Array ) -> Array  -- Bitwise XOR of two Int32 arrays
Array:clamp( min : number, max : number ) -> Array  -- Clamps each element to the given range
Array:clone() -> Array  -- Returns a deep copy of this array
Array:convolve1d( kernel : Array ) -> Array  -- 1D convolution with a kernel array (full output)
Array:convolve2D( kernel : Array ) -> Array  -- 2D convolution with zero-padding
Array:correlate1d( template : Array ) -> Array  -- 1D cross-correlation with a template array (valid output)
Array:countNonZero() -> integer  -- Returns the count of nonzero elements
Array:covariance( other : Array ) -> number  -- Population covariance with another 1D array
Array:cross2d( other : Array ) -> number  -- Signed 2D cross product with another length-2 array
Array:cumsum() -> Array  -- Cumulative sum of all elements (flattened)
Array:diff( order : integer? ) -> Array  -- Discrete difference applied `order` times
Array:dilate( radius : integer ) -> Array  -- Morphological dilation with a diamond structuring element
Array:dot( other : Array ) -> number  -- Dot product of two 1D arrays
Array:erode( radius : integer ) -> Array  -- Morphological erosion with a diamond structuring element
Array:fill( val : number ) -> nil  -- Fills all elements with the given value in-place
Array:get( indices : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the element at the given 1-based indices
Array:getDataType() -> string  -- Returns the element data type name
Array:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns the number of dimensions
Array:getShape() -> table  -- Returns the shape as a table of dimension sizes
Array:getSize() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of elements
Array:isOnGPU() -> boolean  -- Returns false (CPU arrays only)
Array:linsolve( b : Array ) -> Array  -- Solve A·x = b where this array is A (square [n,n]) and b is a 1D vector
Array:luDecompose() -> table  -- Decomposes this square matrix into L and U factors with partial pivoting
Array:matmul( other : Array ) -> Array  -- Matrix multiplication of two 2D arrays
Array:max( axis : integer? ) -> nil  -- Maximum of all elements, or along an axis (1-based)
Array:mean( axis : integer? ) -> nil  -- Mean of all elements, or along an axis (1-based)
Array:min( axis : integer? ) -> nil  -- Minimum of all elements, or along an axis (1-based)
Array:neg() -> Array  -- Returns a new Array with every element negated (multiplied by −1)
Array:normalizeRange( out_min : number, out_max : number ) -> Array  -- Linearly rescale values to [out_min, out_max]
Array:normalizeVec() -> Array  -- L2-normalise a 1D vector
Array:outer( other : Array ) -> Array  -- Outer product of two 1D vectors → 2D array [m, n]
Array:pearsonCorr( other : Array ) -> number  -- Pearson correlation coefficient with another 1D array
Array:percentile( p : number ) -> number  -- Compute the p-th percentile (0–100)
Array:pow( exp : number ) -> Array  -- Raises each element to a scalar exponent
Array:reshape( shape : table ) -> Array  -- Returns a new array with the given shape and the same data
Array:set( indices_and_value : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the element at the given 1-based indices to a value
Array:sobel() -> table  -- Apply Sobel edge detection to a 2D array. Returns {gx=Array, gy=Array}
Array:sqrt() -> Array  -- Element-wise square root
Array:sum( axis : integer? ) -> nil  -- Sum of all elements, or along an axis (1-based)
Array:threshold( val : number ) -> Array  -- Returns a mask array with 1.0 where elements >= val, else 0.0
Array:toTable() -> table  -- Returns all elements as a flat table of numbers
Array:transformPoints( points : Array ) -> Array  -- Apply this 2×2 or 3×3 matrix to an [N,2] points array
Array:transpose() -> Array  -- Returns the transposed 2D array
Array:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name "Array"
Array:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the given name matches "Array" or a parent type
Array:zscore() -> Array  -- Standardise values to zero mean and unit variance
```

## `lurek.dataframe` {#dataframe}

> `lurek.dataframe` — Column-major tabular data with query, analytics, and SQL.
>
> Exposes `DataFrame` (column-oriented table with filter, sort, group, join, pivot)
> and `Database` (named collection of DataFrames with SQL query support). Supports
> CSV/JSON serialization and aggregation functions (sum, mean, min, max, count).

*Coverage: 64/64 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.dataframe.fromBinary( s : string ) -> DataFrame  -- Deserializes a binary LVDF string into a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.fromCSV( s : string ) -> DataFrame  -- Parses a CSV string into a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.fromJSON( s : string ) -> DataFrame  -- Parses a JSON string into a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.fromTable( rows : table ) -> DataFrame  -- Creates a DataFrame from an array of row tables
lurek.dataframe.newDataFrame() -> DataFrame  -- Creates a new empty DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.newDatabase() -> Database  -- Creates a new empty Database
lurek.dataframe.random( defs : table, n : integer, seed : integer? ) -> DataFrame  -- Generates a DataFrame with random data from column definitions
```

### `DataFrame`

Lua-side wrapper around a shared [`DataFrame`].

```lua
DataFrame:addRow( row_tbl : table? ) -> integer  -- Adds a row from an optional table of name-value pairs, returns 1-based index
DataFrame:addRowBatch( rows : table ) -> nil  -- Add multiple rows at once from a table of row tables
DataFrame:clone() -> DataFrame  -- Returns a deep copy of this DataFrame
DataFrame:columns() -> table  -- Returns a table of column names
DataFrame:correlationMatrix() -> DataFrame  -- Compute a correlation matrix for all numeric columns
DataFrame:count() -> integer  -- Returns the row count (alias for nrows)
DataFrame:countBy( col : string|integer ) -> DataFrame  -- Counts distinct values in a column, returns a DataFrame with value and count columns
DataFrame:describe() -> DataFrame  -- Returns descriptive statistics for all numeric columns
DataFrame:dropNil( col : string|integer ) -> DataFrame  -- Removes rows where the given column is nil, returns a new DataFrame
DataFrame:entropy( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Shannon entropy (bits) of the value distribution in a column
DataFrame:fillNil( col : string|integer, val : LuaValue ) -> nil  -- Replaces nil values in a column with the given value
DataFrame:getColumn( col : string|integer ) -> table  -- Returns all values in a column as a table
DataFrame:getColumnAsF64( col : string|integer ) -> table  -- Return a numeric column as a Lua array of numbers (nils → 0/nan)
DataFrame:getRow( row : integer ) -> table  -- Returns a row as a table of name-value pairs
DataFrame:getValue( row : integer, col : string|integer ) -> LuaValue  -- Returns a single cell value
DataFrame:groupBy( col : string|integer ) -> table  -- Groups rows by column value, returns a table of DataFrames keyed by value
DataFrame:head( n : integer? ) -> DataFrame  -- Returns the first n rows (default 5)
DataFrame:max( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the maximum numeric value in a column
DataFrame:mean( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the mean of numeric values in a column
DataFrame:median( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the median of numeric values in a column
DataFrame:merge( other : DataFrame ) -> nil  -- Appends rows from another DataFrame in-place
DataFrame:min( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the minimum numeric value in a column
DataFrame:modeVal( col : string|integer ) -> table|nil  -- Return the most frequent value in a column (nil if empty)
DataFrame:ncols() -> integer  -- Returns the number of columns
DataFrame:nrows() -> integer  -- Returns the number of rows
DataFrame:query( sql_str : string ) -> DataFrame  -- Executes a SQL query against this DataFrame
DataFrame:removeColumn( col : string|integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a column by name or index
DataFrame:removeRow( row : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a row by 1-based index
DataFrame:rename( col : string|integer, new_name : string ) -> nil  -- Renames the column `old_name` to `new_name` in this DataFrame
DataFrame:sample( n : integer, seed : integer? ) -> DataFrame  -- Returns a random sample of n rows
DataFrame:select( cols : string|integer ) -> DataFrame  -- Selects a subset of columns, returns a new DataFrame
DataFrame:setColumnFromF64( col : string|integer, values : table ) -> nil  -- Set a numeric column from a Lua array of numbers
DataFrame:slice( start : integer, end_idx : integer ) -> DataFrame  -- Returns rows from start to end (1-based, inclusive)
DataFrame:stddev( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the population standard deviation of numeric values in a column
DataFrame:sum( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the sum of numeric values in a column
DataFrame:tail( n : integer? ) -> DataFrame  -- Returns the last n rows (default 5)
DataFrame:toBinary( lua : Lua ) -> string  -- Serializes this DataFrame to a binary LVDF string
DataFrame:toCSV() -> string  -- Serializes this DataFrame to a CSV string
DataFrame:toJSON() -> string  -- Serializes this DataFrame to a JSON string
DataFrame:toString() -> string  -- Returns a formatted string table representation
DataFrame:toTable() -> table  -- Converts this DataFrame to a Lua table of row tables
DataFrame:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
DataFrame:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
DataFrame:unique( col : string|integer ) -> table  -- Returns unique values in a column as a table
DataFrame:variance( col : string|integer ) -> number  -- Returns the population variance of numeric values in a column
DataFrame:withEval( col_name : string, expr : string ) -> DataFrame  -- Returns a new DataFrame with an additional computed column named `col_name`
```

### `Database`

Lua-side wrapper around a shared [`Database`].

```lua
Database:clear() -> nil  -- Drops every table from this in-memory database, leaving it empty

Database:tableCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of tables
Database:toJSON() -> string  -- Serializes all tables to a JSON object string
Database:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Database:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

## `lurek.data` {#data}

> `lurek.data` — Binary data manipulation, compression, hashing, and encoding.
>
> Provides struct-style `pack`/`unpack`, binary file I/O via `ByteData`/`DataView`,
> compression (deflate, gzip, zstd, lz4), cryptographic and non-cryptographic hashing,
> base64/hex encoding, TOML parsing, `RingBuffer`, and an ordered-map type.

*Coverage: 58/59 items documented (98%)*

```lua
lurek.data.compress( format : string, data : string, level : integer? ) -> string  -- Compresses data using the given algorithm (deflate, gzip, lz4)
lurek.data.crc32( data : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the CRC-32 checksum of the input data as an integer
lurek.data.decode( format : string, encoded : string ) -> string  -- Decodes encoded text back to binary (base64, hex)
lurek.data.decompress( format : string, data : string ) -> string  -- Decompresses data using the given algorithm (deflate, gzip, lz4)
lurek.data.encode( format : string, data : string ) -> string  -- Encodes binary data using the given format (base64, hex)
lurek.data.encodeToml( tbl : table ) -> string  -- Encodes a Lua table into a TOML string
lurek.data.fromMsgPack( bytes : string ) -> table|nil  -- Deserializes a MessagePack binary string back into a Lua value
lurek.data.getPackedSize( format : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of bytes the given format and values would occupy
lurek.data.hash( algorithm : string, data : string ) -> string  -- Returns the cryptographic hash of the input (md5, sha1, sha256, sha512)
lurek.data.newByteData()
lurek.data.newDataView( data : string, offset : integer?, size : integer? ) -> DataView  -- Creates a read-only windowed view into a byte string
lurek.data.newRingBuffer( capacity : integer ) -> RingBuffer  -- Creates a fixed-capacity ring buffer that can store any Lua value
lurek.data.newWriter() -> DataWriter  -- Creates a new write-cursor for building binary data
lurek.data.pack( format : string ) -> string  -- Packs values into a binary byte string using the format string
lurek.data.parseToml( text : string ) -> table  -- Parses a TOML string into a Lua table
lurek.data.read( format : string, data : string, offset : integer? ) -> table|nil  -- Reads values using the Lurek2D Binary Pack Format
lurek.data.size( format : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the byte size of a Lurek2D Binary Pack Format string
lurek.data.toMsgPack( value : any ) -> string  -- Serializes a Lua value (table, string, number, boolean, or nil) to MessagePack binary
lurek.data.unpack( format : string, data : string, offset : integer? ) -> table|nil  -- Unpacks values from a binary byte string, returning values followed by next offset
lurek.data.write( format : string ) -> string  -- Writes values using the Lurek2D Binary Pack Format
```

### `DataView`

Access structured binary data efficiently without copying.

```lua
DataView:getDouble( offset : integer ) -> number  -- Reads a 64-bit float at the given offset
DataView:getFloat( offset : integer ) -> number  -- Reads a 32-bit float at the given offset
DataView:getInt16( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads a signed 16-bit integer at the given offset
DataView:getInt32( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads a signed 32-bit integer at the given offset
DataView:getInt8( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads a signed 8-bit integer at the given offset
DataView:getSize() -> integer  -- Returns the size of this view in bytes
DataView:getUInt16( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads an unsigned 16-bit integer at the given offset
DataView:getUInt32( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads an unsigned 32-bit integer at the given offset
DataView:getUInt8( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads an unsigned 8-bit integer at the given offset
```

### `DataWriter`

Write-cursor wrapper for the `lurek.data` module.

```lua
DataWriter:len() -> integer  -- Returns the total buffer length
DataWriter:seek( pos : integer )  -- Moves the write cursor to the given position
DataWriter:tell() -> integer  -- Returns the current write cursor position
DataWriter:toBytes() -> string  -- Returns the buffer contents as a Lua string
DataWriter:writeBytes( value : string )  -- Writes raw bytes from a Lua string
DataWriter:writeF32LE( value : number )  -- Writes a 32-bit LE float
DataWriter:writeF64LE( value : number )  -- Writes a 64-bit LE float
DataWriter:writeI16LE( value : integer )  -- Writes a signed 16-bit LE integer
DataWriter:writeI32LE( value : integer )  -- Writes a signed 32-bit LE integer
DataWriter:writeI8( value : integer )  -- Writes a signed 8-bit integer
DataWriter:writeString( value : string )  -- Writes a length-prefixed UTF-8 string (4-byte LE length + bytes)
DataWriter:writeU16BE( value : integer )  -- Writes an unsigned 16-bit BE integer
DataWriter:writeU16LE( value : integer )  -- Writes an unsigned 16-bit LE integer
DataWriter:writeU32LE( value : integer )  -- Writes an unsigned 32-bit LE integer
DataWriter:writeU8( value : integer )  -- Writes an unsigned 8-bit integer
```

### `RingBuffer`

Lua-side fixed-capacity ring buffer that holds any Lua value.

```lua
RingBuffer:capacity() -> integer  -- Returns the maximum number of elements the buffer can hold
RingBuffer:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all elements from the buffer, releasing their registry entries
RingBuffer:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the buffer contains no elements
RingBuffer:isFull() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the buffer has reached its capacity
RingBuffer:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of elements currently in the buffer
RingBuffer:peek() -> table|nil  -- Returns the oldest element without removing it, or nil if empty
RingBuffer:peekNewest() -> table|nil  -- Returns the newest element without removing it, or nil if empty
RingBuffer:pop() -> table|nil  -- Removes and returns the oldest element, or nil if the buffer is empty
RingBuffer:push( value : any ) -> boolean  -- Pushes a value onto the ring buffer
RingBuffer:toTable() -> table  -- Returns all elements as an array table ordered oldest-first
```

### `mlua`

```lua
mlua:clone() -> ByteData  -- Clone the ByteData
mlua:getByte( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Get a byte at the specified offset
mlua:getSize() -> integer  -- Get the size
mlua:getString() -> string  -- Get the string representation
mlua:setByte( offset : integer, value : integer ) -> nil  -- Set a byte at the specified offset
```

## `lurek.image` {#image}

> `lurek.image` — CPU-side pixel-level image manipulation.
>
> Exposes `ImageData` (RGBA pixel buffers), `CompressedImageData` (DXT/BC/ETC),
> `LayeredImage` (multi-layer compositing), `ProvinceGrid` (colour-keyed region maps),
> and `PaletteLUT` (palette-swap look-up tables). All operations run on the CPU;
> upload to GPU is handled by the render layer.

*Coverage: 67/67 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.image.isCompressed( filename : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the file at the given path is a DDS file
lurek.image.loadImage( path : string ) -> ImageData  -- Loads an ImageData from a LIMG binary file
lurek.image.loadLayered( path : string ) -> LayeredImage  -- Loads a LayeredImage from a LIMG binary file
lurek.image.newCompressedData( filename : string ) -> CompressedImageData  -- Loads compressed texture data from a DDS file
lurek.image.newImageData( width_or_filename : integer|string, height : integer? ) -> ImageData  -- Creates a new blank ImageData or loads one from a file
lurek.image.newLayeredImage( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LayeredImage  -- Creates a new empty LayeredImage canvas with no layers
lurek.image.newPaletteLut() -> PaletteLUT  -- Creates a new empty `PaletteLUT` used to remap colours in an image
lurek.image.newProvinceGrid( filename : string ) -> ProvinceGrid  -- Loads a province map PNG and builds an O(1) spatial index with adjacency data
lurek.image.saveImage( imagedata : ImageData, path : string ) -> nil  -- Saves a flat ImageData to a LIMG binary file at the given path
lurek.image.savePNG( imagedata : ImageData, path : string ) -> nil  -- Saves a flat ImageData as a PNG file at the given path
```

### `CompressedImageData`

Lua-side wrapper around [`CompressedImageData`].

```lua
CompressedImageData:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns the width and height of the base mip level
CompressedImageData:getFormat() -> string  -- Returns the compressed format name string
CompressedImageData:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the height of the base mip level in pixels
CompressedImageData:getMipmapCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of mipmap levels stored
CompressedImageData:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the width of the base mip level in pixels
```

### `LayeredImage`

Lua-side wrapper around [`LayeredImage`].

```lua
LayeredImage:addLayer( name : string? ) -> integer  -- Appends a new blank transparent layer on top and returns its 1-based index
LayeredImage:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas height shared by all layers
LayeredImage:getLayer( index : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a copy of the layer's pixel buffer as an ImageData
LayeredImage:getName( index : integer ) -> string  -- Returns the name of a layer
LayeredImage:getOpacity( index : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the opacity of a layer in [0.0, 1.0]
LayeredImage:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas width shared by all layers
LayeredImage:isVisible( index : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a layer is visible
LayeredImage:layerCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of layers in the stack
LayeredImage:merge() -> ImageData  -- Flattens all visible layers into a single ImageData using Porter-Duff "over" compositing
LayeredImage:removeLayer( index : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes the layer at the given 1-based index. Returns true on success
LayeredImage:save( path : string ) -> nil  -- Saves the layered image to a LIMG binary file at the given path
LayeredImage:setName( index : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Renames the layer at the given index to the new name string
LayeredImage:setOpacity( index : integer, opacity : number ) -> boolean  -- Sets the opacity of a layer. Value is clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
LayeredImage:setVisible( index : integer, visible : boolean ) -> boolean  -- Shows or hides a layer during compositing
LayeredImage:swapLayers( a : integer, b : integer ) -> boolean  -- Swaps two layers by their 1-based indices, changing their compositing order
```

## `lurek.graph` {#graph}

> `lurek.graph` — Directed graph with item-flow simulation.
>
> Provides a node/edge graph model with typed item transport, overflow policies,
> conversion rules, demand/supply links, and event callbacks (`itemEnter`,
> `itemLeave`, etc.). Also exposes graph-level pathfinding (Dijkstra, BFS, A*)
> through the `find*` methods on the `Graph` userdata.

*Coverage: 112/112 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.graph.newGraph() -> Graph  -- Creates a new empty directed graph for item flow simulation
```

### `Edge`

Lua handle for an edge inside a `Graph`.

```lua
Edge:addAllowedType( t : string ) -> nil  -- Adds an item type to the edge allow-list
Edge:clearAllowedTypes() -> nil  -- Clears the edge allow-list so all item types are permitted
Edge:getCapacity() -> integer  -- Returns the edge capacity (-1 = unlimited)
Edge:getCooldown() -> number  -- Returns the cooldown duration in seconds
Edge:getFrom() -> Node  -- Returns the source node handle
Edge:getItemsInTransit() -> table  -- Returns a table of GraphItem handles currently in transit on this edge
Edge:getSpeedModifier() -> number  -- Returns the speed modifier applied to items in transit
Edge:getThroughput() -> number  -- Returns items per second this edge can transfer
Edge:getTo() -> Node  -- Returns the destination node handle
Edge:getTravelTime() -> number  -- Returns the travel time in seconds for items on this edge
Edge:getType() -> string  -- Returns the edge type string
Edge:getWeight() -> number  -- Returns the pathfinding weight of this edge
Edge:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the edge is active
Edge:isBidirectional() -> boolean  -- Returns true if items can travel the edge in either direction
Edge:isItemTypeAllowed( t : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the given item type is allowed on this edge
Edge:isOnCooldown() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the edge is currently on cooldown
Edge:removeAllowedType( t : string ) -> boolean  -- Removes an item type from the edge allow-list
Edge:setActive( a : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets the active state of this edge
Edge:setBidirectional( b : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets whether items can travel the edge in either direction
Edge:setCapacity( c : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the edge capacity (-1 = unlimited)
Edge:setCooldown( c : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the cooldown duration in seconds
Edge:setSpeedModifier( m : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the speed modifier applied to items in transit
Edge:setThroughput( t : number ) -> nil  -- Sets items per second this edge can transfer
Edge:setTravelTime( t : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the travel time in seconds for items on this edge
Edge:setType( t : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the edge type string
Edge:setWeight( w : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the pathfinding weight of this edge
Edge:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name "GraphEdge"
Edge:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the given name matches "GraphEdge" or a parent type
```

### `Graph`

Lua wrapper around a directed `Graph` with event callback registry.

```lua
Graph:astar( from_node : Node, to_node : Node ) -> table?  -- Finds the shortest path between two nodes using A*
Graph:colorGraph() -> table  -- Assigns each node the smallest non-negative integer colour not shared with any
Graph:getComponents() -> table  -- Returns weakly connected components as a table of tables of Node handles
Graph:getEdgeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of edges in the graph
Graph:getEdges() -> table  -- Returns a table of all Edge handles
Graph:getItemCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the graph
Graph:getItems() -> table  -- Returns a table of all GraphItem handles
Graph:getNeighbors( node_ud : Node ) -> table  -- Returns a table of direct neighbor Node handles
Graph:getNodeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of nodes in the graph
Graph:getNodes() -> table  -- Returns a table of all Node handles
Graph:getStats() -> table  -- Returns a statistics snapshot table
Graph:hasCycle() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the graph contains a directed cycle
Graph:hasEdge( edge_ud : Edge ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the edge exists in the graph
Graph:hasItem( item_ud : GraphItem ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the item exists in the graph
Graph:hasNode( node_ud : Node ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the node exists in the graph
Graph:isBipartite() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` when the graph can be 2-coloured (bipartite check via BFS)

Graph:removeEdge( edge_ud : Edge ) -> boolean  -- Removes an edge from the graph
Graph:removeItem( item_ud : GraphItem ) -> boolean  -- Removes an item from the graph entirely
Graph:removeNode( node_ud : Node ) -> boolean  -- Removes a node from the graph
Graph:step() -> nil  -- Runs one discrete simulation step and fires event callbacks
Graph:tickParallel( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances simulation by dt seconds using a parallelised decay phase
Graph:topologicalSort() -> table?  -- Returns a topologically sorted table of Node handles, or nil if a cycle exists
Graph:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Graph:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
Graph:update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances simulation by dt seconds and fires event callbacks
```

```lua
DepthSorter:sort() -> nil  -- Sorts all registered callbacks by depth ascending
```

## `lurek.log` {#log}

> Structured game-level logging API (`lurek.log.*`).
>
> Exposes convenience logging functions so Lua scripts can emit messages at
> specific severity levels and query or change the active log level at
> runtime. Also supports configurable output sinks (file, memory ring buffer)
> in addition to the default stderr channel, similar to Python's `logging`
> module handlers.

*Coverage: 18/18 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.log.addSink( config : table ) -> integer  -- Registers a new output sink. Returns its numeric id
lurek.log.clearSinks() -> nil  -- Removes all registered sinks (the default stderr channel is unaffected)
lurek.log.debug( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a debug-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.debug_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a debug structured log message. Shorthand for `struct("debug", ...)`
lurek.log.error( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits an error-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.error_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits an error structured log message. Shorthand for `struct("error", ...)`
lurek.log.flushFile( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Flushes the OS write buffer for a file sink
lurek.log.getLevel() -> string  -- Returns the name of the currently active minimum log level
lurek.log.info( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits an info-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.info_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits an info structured log message. Shorthand for `struct("info", ...)`
lurek.log.listSinks() -> table  -- Returns a table describing all registered sinks
lurek.log.print( level : string, message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a log message at the specified level. Also dispatches to sinks
lurek.log.readMemory( id : integer, drain : boolean? ) -> table?  -- Reads entries from a memory sink. If drain=true the buffer is cleared
lurek.log.removeSink( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes a sink by id. Returns true if one was removed
lurek.log.setLevel( level : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the minimum severity level for the default log channel
lurek.log.struct( level : string, message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a structured log message with key-value fields
lurek.log.warn( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a warn-severity log message. Also dispatches to configured sinks
lurek.log.warn_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a warn structured log message. Shorthand for `struct("warn", ...)`
```

---

## `lurek.debugbridge` {#debugbridge}

> Registers the `lurek.debugbridge.*` TCP debug server API.
>
> Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
> External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
> control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
> Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

*Coverage: 14/14 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.debugbridge.broadcast( event : string, json_data : string )  -- Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients
lurek.debugbridge.capturePrint( msg : string, source : string?, line : integer? )  -- Captures a print message and broadcasts it to connected clients
lurek.debugbridge.clearPrintHistory()  -- Clears the print history
lurek.debugbridge.getClientCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of connected TCP clients
lurek.debugbridge.getPerformance() -> table  -- Returns performance statistics
lurek.debugbridge.getPort() -> integer  -- Returns the server port (0 if not running)
lurek.debugbridge.getPrintHistory( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns the print history
lurek.debugbridge.isRunning() -> bool  -- Returns whether the server is currently running
lurek.debugbridge.isScreenshotRequested() -> bool  -- Returns whether a screenshot is currently requested
lurek.debugbridge.poll() -> table|nil  -- Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients
lurek.debugbridge.requestScreenshot( scale : integer? )  -- Flags a screenshot request for the next frame
lurek.debugbridge.setMaxPrintHistory( max : integer )  -- Sets the maximum print history size
lurek.debugbridge.start( port : u16? ) -> boolean  -- Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port
lurek.debugbridge.stop()  -- Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections
```

---

> Provides engine-integrated documentation management: scanning runtime bindings,
> loading TOML doc catalogs, validating completeness, computing quality metrics,
> exporting structured data for VS Code extension IntelliSense, and
> a lightweight schema validator for game-developer data validation.

*Coverage: 75/75 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.docs.checkStaleness( catalog_ud : userdata, source_dir : string ) -> table  -- Compare catalog entries against source files in a directory for staleness
lurek.docs.coverage( catalog_ud : userdata? ) -> integer  -- Return (documented_count, total_live_count) coverage tuple
lurek.docs.coverageModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? ) -> integer  -- Return (documented_count, total_live_count) for a single module
lurek.docs.describe( qualified_name : string, description : string )  -- Inject or update a description for a named API entry
lurek.docs.exportAll( catalog_ud : userdata, output_dir : string )  -- Export completions.json, hover.json, and signatures.json to a directory
lurek.docs.exportCheatsheet( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export a one-line-per-function plain-text cheatsheet
lurek.docs.exportCompletions( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export VS Code IntelliSense completions JSON to a file
lurek.docs.exportHover( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export VS Code hover JSON to a file
lurek.docs.exportMarkdown( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export a Markdown API reference file
lurek.docs.exportSignatures( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export VS Code signature-help JSON to a file
lurek.docs.getCatalog()  -- Return the current internal catalog as an ApiCatalog userdata
lurek.docs.loadAll( directory : string ) -> ApiCatalog  -- Load all .toml files in a directory and merge into a single ApiCatalog
lurek.docs.loadToml( path : string ) -> ApiCatalog  -- Load a TOML doc file into an ApiCatalog
lurek.docs.quality( catalog_ud : userdata? ) -> table  -- Calculate quality metrics for a catalog or the internal catalog
lurek.docs.qualityModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? ) -> table  -- Calculate quality metrics for a single module
lurek.docs.reflectLive( ns : string? ) -> table  -- Walks the live lurek.* Lua table and returns a structured reflection of all
lurek.docs.reflectTable( tbl : table, name : string? ) -> table  -- Reflects any Lua table, returning a structure describing its keys,
lurek.docs.resetCatalog()  -- Clear all entries from the internal catalog
lurek.docs.scan( opts : table? ) -> ApiCatalog  -- Scan the lurek.* namespace to build an API catalog from live bindings
lurek.docs.scanModule( module_name : string ) -> ApiCatalog  -- Scan a single module's bindings
lurek.docs.schema( rules : table, name : string? ) -> userdata  -- Creates a Schema validator from a rules table
lurek.docs.setParamInfo( qualified_name : string, params : table )  -- Set the parameter metadata for a catalog entry
lurek.docs.setReturnInfo( qualified_name : string, returns : table )  -- Set the return type metadata for a catalog entry
lurek.docs.validate( catalog_ud : userdata? ) -> ValidationReport  -- Validate catalog completeness against the live lurek.* bindings
lurek.docs.validateModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? ) -> ValidationReport  -- Validate a single module against the live lurek.<module>.* bindings
```

### `ApiCatalog`

Wraps a catalog snapshot of API entries for Lua access.

```lua
ApiCatalog:entryCount( module : string? ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries, optionally scoped to a module
ApiCatalog:filter( predicate : function ) -> ApiCatalog  -- Returns a new catalog containing only entries for which predicate returns true
ApiCatalog:getEntries( module : string? ) -> table  -- Returns all entries, optionally filtered to a single module
ApiCatalog:getEntry( qualified_name : string ) -> nil  -- Returns a single entry by qualified name, or nil
ApiCatalog:getModules() -> table  -- Returns a sorted list of module names present in the catalog
ApiCatalog:getTypeMethods( qualified_name : string ) -> table  -- Returns entries that are methods of the given type qualified name
ApiCatalog:getTypes( module_name : string ) -> table  -- Returns the names of all entries with kind "type" in the given module
ApiCatalog:merge( other : userdata ) -> ApiCatalog  -- Returns a new catalog that is the union of this and another catalog, with other overriding duplicates
ApiCatalog:search( query : string ) -> table  -- Returns a table of entries whose name, qualified name, or description contains query
ApiCatalog:toJSON() -> string  -- Serialises the catalog to a pretty-printed JSON string
ApiCatalog:toTable() -> table  -- Converts the catalog to a plain Lua table array
```

### `DocEntry`

Wraps a single doc entry for Lua access.

```lua
DocEntry:getDeprecated() -> string?  -- Returns the deprecation message, or nil
DocEntry:getDescription() -> string  -- Returns the human-readable description text for this documentation entry
DocEntry:getExample() -> string?  -- Returns the example snippet, or nil
DocEntry:getKind() -> string  -- Returns the kind tag for this entry (e.g. `'function'`, `'method'`, `'class'`)
DocEntry:getModule() -> string  -- Returns the Lua module name this entry belongs to (e.g. `'lurek.math'`)
DocEntry:getName() -> string  -- Returns the symbol name for this documentation entry
DocEntry:getParameters() -> table  -- Returns the parameters as a table of `{name, type, description, optional, default?}` records
DocEntry:getQualifiedName() -> string  -- Returns the qualified name
DocEntry:getReturns() -> table  -- Returns the return values as a table of `{type, description}` records
DocEntry:getScore() -> number  -- Returns the quality score in [0,1]
DocEntry:getSince() -> string?  -- Returns the since version string, or nil
DocEntry:hasDescription() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry has a non-empty description
DocEntry:hasExample() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry has an example snippet
DocEntry:hasParameters() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry has at least one parameter
DocEntry:hasReturnType() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry declares at least one return type
```

### `QualityReport`

Wraps documentation quality metrics for Lua access.

```lua
QualityReport:getBest( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns up to count entries with the highest quality scores
QualityReport:getByGrade( grade : string ) -> table  -- Returns entries whose grade exactly matches the given letter grade
QualityReport:getGrade() -> string  -- Returns the letter grade for the overall score
QualityReport:getModuleScores() -> table  -- Returns a table mapping module name to its average quality score
QualityReport:getOverallScore() -> number  -- Returns the overall quality score in [0,1]
QualityReport:getSummary() -> string  -- Returns a multi-line human-readable summary of quality by module
QualityReport:getWorst( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns up to count entries with the lowest quality scores
QualityReport:toJSON() -> string  -- Serialises the quality report to a pretty-printed JSON string
QualityReport:toTable() -> table  -- Converts the quality report to a plain Lua table
```

### `Schema`

Lua wrapper for a runtime data-validation schema.

```lua
Schema:assert( data : table ) -> nil  -- Validates data and throws a Lua error on failure with all error messages joined
Schema:check( data : table ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the data passes all schema rules
Schema:getFields() -> table  -- Returns a table of declared field names
Schema:getName() -> string  -- Returns the name identifier of this API schema group
Schema:validate( data : table ) -> nil  -- Validates a Lua table against the schema
```

### `ValidationReport`

Wraps a validation report for Lua access.

```lua
ValidationReport:getIncomplete() -> table  -- Returns the list of qualified names whose catalog entry is incomplete
ValidationReport:getMissing() -> table  -- Returns the list of qualified names present in the live API but missing from the catalog
ValidationReport:getPhantom() -> table  -- Returns the list of qualified names in the catalog that are not present in the live API
ValidationReport:getSummary() -> string  -- Returns a single-line summary of the validation results
ValidationReport:incompleteCount() -> integer  -- Returns the count of incomplete entries
ValidationReport:isValid() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the report has no missing entries
ValidationReport:missingCount() -> integer  -- Returns the count of missing entries
ValidationReport:phantomCount() -> integer  -- Returns the count of phantom entries
ValidationReport:toJSON() -> string  -- Serialises the report to a pretty-printed JSON string
ValidationReport:toTable() -> table  -- Converts the report to a plain Lua table
```
