## `lurek.patterns` {#patterns}

> Registers the `lurek.patterns.*` software design patterns API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for reusable patterns:
> EventBus (prioritized pub-sub), ObjectPool (object reuse), CommandStack
> (undo/redo), ServiceLocator (DI container), Factory (named constructors),
> and SimpleState (finite state machine).

*Coverage: 170/170 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.patterns.newBlackboard( name : string? ) -> Blackboard  -- Creates a new Blackboard shared key-value store
lurek.patterns.newCommandStack( max_size : integer? ) -> CommandStack  -- Creates a new CommandStack instance
lurek.patterns.newDebounce( wait : number ) -> Debounce  -- Creates a trailing-edge debounce that fires after the input stream is idle for wait seconds
lurek.patterns.newEventBus( name : string? ) -> EventBus  -- Creates a new EventBus instance
lurek.patterns.newFactory() -> Factory  -- Creates a new Factory instance
lurek.patterns.newFunnel( window : number, max_entries : integer?, name : string? ) -> Funnel  -- Creates a time-windowed event aggregator. window=0 means flush on every push
lurek.patterns.newList() -> List  -- Creates an ordered, resizable list
lurek.patterns.newMediator() -> Mediator  -- Creates a new named-channel message broker
lurek.patterns.newObjectPool() -> ObjectPool  -- Creates a new ObjectPool instance
lurek.patterns.newObserver( name : string? ) -> Observer  -- Creates a new reactive property Observer
lurek.patterns.newPriorityQueue( name : string? ) -> PriorityQueue  -- Creates a stable priority-ordered task queue
lurek.patterns.newQueue( capacity : integer? ) -> Queue  -- Creates a FIFO queue. capacity=0 means unlimited
lurek.patterns.newRelationshipManager() -> RelationshipManager  -- Creates a new entity relationship manager
lurek.patterns.newRing( capacity : integer, name : string? ) -> Ring  -- Creates a fixed-capacity circular history buffer
lurek.patterns.newServiceLocator() -> ServiceLocator  -- Creates a new ServiceLocator instance
lurek.patterns.newSet() -> Set  -- Creates an unordered set that rejects duplicate values (by string key)
lurek.patterns.newSimpleState() -> SimpleState  -- Creates a new SimpleState finite state machine instance
lurek.patterns.newStack( capacity : integer? ) -> Stack  -- Creates a LIFO stack. capacity=0 means unlimited
lurek.patterns.newStrategy() -> Strategy  -- Creates a new strategy registry
lurek.patterns.newThrottle( interval : number ) -> Throttle  -- Creates a leading-edge rate limiter that fires at most once per interval seconds
```

### `Blackboard`

Lua wrapper for the Blackboard pattern.

```lua
Blackboard:clear( key : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a fact from the blackboard
Blackboard:clearAll() -> nil  -- Clears all facts from the blackboard
Blackboard:get( key : string ) -> boolean|number|string|nil  -- Gets a fact from the blackboard. Returns nil if not set
Blackboard:getRevision() -> integer  -- Returns the monotonic revision counter (incremented on every write)
Blackboard:has( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the key has a non-nil value
Blackboard:keys() -> table  -- Returns all set fact keys as a table
Blackboard:set( key : string, value : any ) -> nil  -- Sets a fact on the blackboard. Accepts boolean, number, or string values
Blackboard:snapshot() -> table  -- Returns all facts as a flat key→value table
Blackboard:unwatch( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a watcher subscription by id
Blackboard:watch( key : string, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Subscribes to changes on a specific key (or "*" for all changes)
```

### `CommandStack`

Lua wrapper for the CommandStack pattern.

```lua
CommandStack:canRedo() -> boolean  -- Returns true if there is a command available to redo
CommandStack:canUndo() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the most recent command can be undone
CommandStack:clearAll() -> nil  -- Clears all command history, releasing Lua registry values
CommandStack:execute( name : string, exec_fn : function, undo_fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Executes a named command and records it in undo/redo history
CommandStack:getCurrentName() -> string?  -- Returns the name of the most recently executed command, or nil
CommandStack:getHistorySize() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of recorded commands (undo + redo)
CommandStack:redo() -> boolean  -- Re-executes the next undone command. Returns true if successful
CommandStack:undo() -> boolean  -- Undoes the most recent command. Returns true if successful
```

### `Debounce`

Lua wrapper for the Debounce pattern.

```lua
Debounce:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels the pending trigger without firing
Debounce:getFireCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of times this debounce has fired
Debounce:isPending() -> boolean  -- Returns true when a trigger is pending
Debounce:onFire( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback invoked when the debounce fires
Debounce:trigger() -> nil  -- Records an input event, resetting the idle timer
Debounce:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the idle timer by dt seconds; fires the callback if idle wait expired
```

### `EventBus`

Lua wrapper for the EventBus pattern.

```lua
EventBus:clear( event : string ) -> nil  -- Removes all listeners for a specific event
EventBus:clearAll() -> nil  -- Removes all listeners on this EventBus
EventBus:emit( args : MultiValue ) -> nil  -- Dispatches an event, calling all registered listeners in priority order
EventBus:getEvents() -> table  -- Returns all event names that have at least one listener
EventBus:getListenerCount( event : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of listeners registered for an event
EventBus:off( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a previously registered event listener by subscription ID
EventBus:on( event : string, callback : function, priority : integer? ) -> integer  -- Registers a listener callback for an event
```

### `Factory`

Lua wrapper for the Factory pattern.

```lua
Factory:alias( alias : string, canonical : string ) -> nil  -- Registers an alias pointing to an existing canonical type name
Factory:clearAll() -> nil  -- Removes all registered type constructors and aliases
Factory:create( args : MultiValue ) -> table|userdata  -- Creates an instance of the named type by invoking its constructor
Factory:getTypes() -> table  -- Returns a table of all registered type names
Factory:has( type_name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the named type (or alias) is registered
Factory:register( type_name : string, ctor : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a named type constructor function
Factory:remove( type_name : string ) -> nil  -- Unregisters a type constructor (and any aliases pointing to it)
```

### `Funnel`

Lua wrapper for the Funnel (event aggregator) pattern.

```lua
Funnel:discard() -> nil  -- Discards all buffered entries without flushing
Funnel:flush() -> nil  -- Manually flushes all pending entries, invoking the onFlush callback
Funnel:getFlushCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of flushes performed
Funnel:onFlush( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Sets a callback invoked when the funnel flushes. Receives a table of {tag, value} entries
Funnel:pendingCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of buffered entries not yet flushed
Funnel:push( tag : string, value : number? ) -> nil  -- Adds an event to the funnel. Immediately flushes if max_entries reached or window is 0
Funnel:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the window timer by dt seconds; flushes when window expires
```

### `List`

Lua wrapper for an ordered, resizable list.

```lua
List:add( value : any ) -> nil  -- Appends a value to the end of the list
List:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all values from the list
List:contains( value : any ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the list contains a value equal to the given Lua value (string/number/boolean)
List:get( index : integer ) -> table|nil  -- Returns the value at a 1-based index, or nil
List:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the list is empty
List:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the list
List:remove( index : integer ) -> table|nil  -- Removes and returns the value at a 1-based index
List:set( index : integer, value : any ) -> nil  -- Replaces the value at a 1-based index
List:toArray() -> table  -- Returns all items as a Lua table
```

### `Mediator`

Lua wrapper for the Mediator pattern.

```lua
Mediator:broadcast( args : MultiValue ) -> nil  -- Dispatches a message to all handlers across all channels
Mediator:channels() -> table  -- Returns all registered channel names
Mediator:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all channels and handlers
Mediator:handlerCount( channel : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of handlers on a channel
Mediator:off( channel : string, id : integer ) -> nil  -- Unregisters a handler by ID
Mediator:on( channel : string, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Registers a handler callback on a channel; returns handler ID
Mediator:removeChannel( channel : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a channel and all its handlers
Mediator:send( channel : string, args : MultiValue ) -> nil  -- Dispatches a message to all handlers on a channel
```

### `ObjectPool`

Lua wrapper for the ObjectPool pattern.

```lua
ObjectPool:acquire() -> string|number|boolean|table|nil  -- Acquires an available object from the pool; returns nil if empty
ObjectPool:add( value : any ) -> nil  -- Inserts a pre-built object into the available pool
ObjectPool:clearAll() -> nil  -- Clears all objects from the pool, releasing Lua registry values
ObjectPool:getActiveCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of currently active (acquired) objects
ObjectPool:getAvailableCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of available (idle) objects in the pool
ObjectPool:getTotalCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of tracked objects (active + available)
ObjectPool:release( value : any ) -> nil  -- Returns an object to the available pool
```

### `Observer`

Lua wrapper for the Observer pattern.

```lua
Observer:get( key : string ) -> string|number|boolean|table|nil  -- Gets a property value, or nil if not set
Observer:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of active subscriptions
Observer:set( key : string, value : any ) -> nil  -- Sets a property value and fires subscribed watchers
```

## `lurek.physics` {#collision}

> `lurek.physics` — Lightweight stateless geometric collision helpers.
>
> These pure-math functions perform fast overlap detection without requiring a
> full physics world. Suitable for RPG, puzzle, or visual-novel games that only
> need simple overlap detection, not rigid-body simulation.

*Coverage: 4/4 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.physics.testAABB( ax : number, ay : number, aw : number, ah : number, bx : number, by : number, bw : number, bh : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when two axis-aligned bounding boxes overlap
lurek.physics.testCircleAABB( cx : number, cy : number, cr : number, ax : number, ay : number, aw : number, ah : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when a circle overlaps an AABB
lurek.physics.testCircles( ax : number, ay : number, ar : number, bx : number, by : number, br : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when two circles overlap
lurek.physics.testPoint( px : number, py : number, ax : number, ay : number, aw : number, ah : number ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when point (px, py) lies inside the AABB
```

## `lurek.effect` {#effect}

> `lurek.effect` — Composable visual effects: post-processing pipeline and screen overlays.
>
> Exposes `PostFxEffect` (individual shader passes), `PostFxStack` (ordered chains),
> `ImageEffect` (named preset bundles), `Overlay` (screen-space weather/vignette),
> and `ScreenTransition` (fade/wipe between scenes). Also provides render-to-texture
> feedback loops for motion-trail effects.

*Coverage: 142/142 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.effect.getEffectTypes() -> table  -- Returns the list of all built-in effect type names
lurek.effect.getShaderErrorDisplay() -> boolean  -- Returns whether shader error display is currently enabled

lurek.effect.newEffect( type_name : string ) -> PostFxEffect  -- Creates a new built-in post-processing effect by type name
lurek.effect.newImageEffect( args : MultiValue ) -> ImageEffect  -- Creates a new per-image effect chain. Accepts:
lurek.effect.newOverlay( width : integer, height : integer ) -> Overlay  -- Creates a new screen overlay controller for weather, flash, shake, and fade effects
lurek.effect.newPass( shader_id : integer ) -> PostFxEffect  -- Creates a custom-shader post-processing effect (alias for newCustomEffect)
lurek.effect.newPresetStack( name : string, width : integer?, height : integer? ) -> PostFxStack  -- Creates a pre-configured effect stack from a named preset
lurek.effect.newStack( width : integer?, height : integer? ) -> PostFxStack  -- Creates a new post-processing pipeline stack
lurek.effect.newTransition( kind : string?, duration : number?, color : table? ) -> ScreenTransition  -- Creates a new screen-transition controller. `kind` is one of:
lurek.effect.setShaderErrorDisplay( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables the overlay that renders shader compile errors as red text
```

## `lurek.light` {#light}

> `lurek.light` — 2D lighting, shadow occluders, and ambient control.

> Manages `Light` handles (point / directional / spot) and `Occluder` handles

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Pipeline`] DAG with scheduler and Lua callback registry.

```lua

Pipeline:isRunning() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the pipeline is currently running asynchronously

Pipeline:run( context : table? ) -> table  -- Executes the pipeline synchronously in topological order

Pipeline:setName( name : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the pipeline's name

Pipeline:setOnStepError( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback to invoke each time a step fails
Pipeline:toAscii() -> string  -- Returns a multi-line ASCII string visualising the pipeline DAG
Pipeline:toTable() -> table  -- Serialises the pipeline definition to a Lua table (no callbacks)
Pipeline:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Pipeline:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns the type identifier string of this pipeline stage object
```

```lua
Step:setRetryDelay( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the delay in seconds between retry attempts
Step:setTag( tag : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the tag on this step for grouping and filtering
Step:setTimeout( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Stores a timeout in seconds in the step's metadata
Step:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name "PipelineStep"
```

### `Canvas`

Lua-side handle to an off-screen render target stored in SharedState.

```lua
Canvas:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns width and height of this canvas
Canvas:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the height of this canvas in pixels
Canvas:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the width of this canvas in pixels
Canvas:release() -> boolean  -- Releases GPU framebuffer memory for this canvas
Canvas:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Canvas:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
```

### `DrawLayer`

Lua-side z-ordered draw queue. Callbacks are sorted by z and called on `flush()`.

```lua
DrawLayer:clear() -> void  -- Removes all queued callbacks without calling them
DrawLayer:flush() -> nil  -- Sorts and calls all queued callbacks, then empties the queue
DrawLayer:getCount() -> number  -- Returns the number of queued callbacks
DrawLayer:queue( z : number, fn : function ) -> nil  -- Queues a draw callback at the given z-order
DrawLayer:type() -> string  -- Returns the string type identifier of this draw layer (e.g. `'sprite'`)
DrawLayer:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is an instance of the given type name
```

### `Font`

Lua-side handle to a loaded font stored in SharedState.

```lua
Font:getAscent() -> number  -- Returns the ascent of this font in pixels
Font:getDescent() -> number  -- Returns the descent of this font in pixels
Font:getHeight() -> number  -- Returns the line height of this font
Font:getLineHeight() -> number  -- Returns the line height multiplier of this font
Font:getWidth( text : string ) -> number  -- Returns the rendered width of the given text string
Font:getWrap( text : string, limit : number ) -> nil  -- Wraps text to the given width and returns the lines
Font:release() -> boolean  -- Releases this font and frees its atlas memory
Font:setLineHeight( height : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the line height multiplier for this font
Font:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
Font:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
```


## `lurek.save` {#save}

> `lurek.save` - Slot-based save/load system with collectors, schema versioning, and auto-save.

*Coverage: 23/23 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.save.newSaveManager() -> SaveManager  -- Creates a new SaveManager for slot-based save/load operations
```

### `SaveManager`

Lua-side wrapper around [`SaveManager`] with per-module callback storage.

```lua
SaveManager:collect() -> table  -- Collects data from all registered collectors into a table with metadata
SaveManager:delete( slot : string ) -> nil  -- Deletes a save file for the given slot
SaveManager:disableAutoSave() -> nil  -- Disables automatic periodic saving; manual `write()` calls still work
SaveManager:exists( slot : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a save file exists for the given slot
SaveManager:getSchemaVersion() -> integer  -- Returns the current schema version
SaveManager:getSlotInfo( slot : string ) -> table?  -- Returns metadata for a single slot, or nil if not found
SaveManager:getSlots() -> table  -- Returns a list of all save slots with metadata
SaveManager:getSummary() -> string  -- Returns the current summary string
SaveManager:isCompressed() -> boolean  -- Returns whether compression is currently enabled
SaveManager:isDirty() -> boolean  -- Returns whether data has been modified since the last save or load
SaveManager:load( slot : string ) -> nil  -- Loads data from a slot file, applies migrations, and restores
SaveManager:markDirty() -> nil  -- Marks data as modified since the last save or load
SaveManager:onAfterLoad( func : function? ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback that fires after every successful load operation
SaveManager:onBeforeSave( func : function? ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback that fires before every save operation
SaveManager:reset() -> nil  -- Resets all state, removing callbacks and clearing the manager
SaveManager:restore( data : table ) -> nil  -- Restores data from a table, applying migrations and calling restorers
SaveManager:save( slot : string ) -> nil  -- Collects data and writes it to a slot file
SaveManager:setCompress( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables LZ4 compression for saved data
SaveManager:setSchemaVersion( version : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the current schema version for new saves
SaveManager:setSummary( summary : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the summary string included in save metadata
SaveManager:unregister( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a named module and its callbacks
SaveManager:update( dt : number ) -> string?  -- Advances the auto-save timer, returning the slot name if a save should trigger
```

## `lurek.tween` {#tween}

> `lurek.tween` — thin Lua registration wrapper for the property tween system.
>
> # Purpose

*Coverage: 35/35 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.tween.cancelAll() -> nil  -- Cancels all active tweens, sequences, parallels, and springs immediately
lurek.tween.delay( seconds : number, fn : function ) -> TweenSequence  -- Creates a no-op tween that waits `seconds`, then optionally calls `callback`
lurek.tween.getActiveCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of currently active tween objects (tweens + seqs + pars)
lurek.tween.getEasingNames() -> table  -- Returns a list of all available easing names (built-in + custom)
lurek.tween.newState( duration : number, easing : string ) -> TweenState  -- Creates a standalone tween timing state without registering it with the engine
lurek.tween.parallel() -> TweenParallel  -- Creates an empty TweenParallel. Add entries with :tween() or :add(tween),
lurek.tween.registerEasing( name : string, fn : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a custom easing function under `name`. `fn(t)` receives 0..1, returns 0..1
lurek.tween.sequence() -> TweenSequence  -- Creates an empty TweenSequence. Add steps with :tween(), :delay(), :callback(),
lurek.tween.spring( target_table : table, fields_table : table, opts : table? ) -> Spring  -- Creates a physics-based spring animation that drives named fields on `target_table`
lurek.tween.to( target : table, fields : table, duration : number, easing : string? ) -> Tween  -- Sugar for `tween()` with `target` first — natural read order
lurek.tween.tween( duration : number, target : table, fields : table, easing : string ) -> Tween  -- Creates a new property tween and registers it for automatic updating
lurek.tween.update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances all active tweens, sequences, and parallels by `dt` seconds
```

### `Spring`

Lua-side spring handle: wraps [`SpringSystem`] and a registry reference to the target table.

```lua
Spring:cancel() -> nil  -- Stops the spring. The engine will drop it on the next `update(dt)` call
Spring:getPosition( field : string ) -> number?  -- Returns the current interpolated position for the named field, or `nil`
Spring:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if the spring has not been cancelled or settled
Spring:isSettled() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` when all spring axes have converged within `precision`
Spring:setDamping( value : number ) -> nil  -- Updates the damping coefficient on all axes
Spring:setStiffness( value : number ) -> nil  -- Updates the stiffness constant on all axes
Spring:setTarget( fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Updates target values for all fields present in `fields_table`
Spring:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the spring by `dt` seconds and writes positions to the target table
```

### `Tween`

A managed interpolation from start to end values over time.

```lua
Tween:getProgress() -> number  -- Returns raw 0..1 playback progress (not eased, not accounting for yoyo)
Tween:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the tween is still running (not completed or cancelled)
Tween:pause() -> nil  -- Pauses this tween; time stops advancing but the tween is not cancelled
Tween:resume() -> nil  -- Resumes a paused tween, continuing from the position where it was paused
Tween:setRepeat( n : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the number of extra play cycles after the first (0 = play once, -1 = infinite)
Tween:setYoyo( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables yoyo (ping-pong) on each repeat cycle
```

### `TweenParallel`

A group of animations that run simultaneously over the same duration.

```lua
TweenParallel:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels the parallel group immediately
TweenParallel:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the parallel is running and not yet complete
```

### `TweenSequence`

A chained sequence of animations that run one after another.

```lua
TweenSequence:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels the sequence and stops all pending steps
TweenSequence:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the sequence has been started and has not yet completed
```

### `TweenState`

Lua-side wrapper around the pure-Rust [`TweenState`] timing core.

```lua
TweenState:isComplete() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the tween state has completed
TweenState:lerp( start : number, finish : number ) -> number  -- Interpolates from `start` to `finish` using the eased tween progress
TweenState:reset() -> nil  -- Resets the tween state to elapsed time zero
TweenState:t() -> number  -- Returns the raw 0..1 playback progress
TweenState:tick( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the tween state by `dt` seconds
```
